title: lucuma laurifolia
intro: Lucuma laurifolia, also known as the yellow lucuma or lucuma tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is native to the Andes mountains and is a small evergreen tree with a broad, rounded crown and a straight, grayish-brown trunk. The medicinal properties of Lucuma laurifolia include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which are derived from its bark and leaves. The health benefits of consuming Lucuma laurifolia include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and support immune function, and it is often consumed as a tea or used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures.
